source_formulae: P0R05953: The Psi-Field as a Negentropy Source and the Landauer Cost of Coherence

P0R05954: The Psi-field acts as an information reservoir (a Maxwell's Demon), utilising information to reduce local thermodynamic entropy (S<sub>Thermo</sub>) in the biological substrate. The rate of negentropy injection (N<sub>Psi</sub>) is bounded by the mutual information (I) between the Psi-field and the substrate (B):

specs.section_path: Structural Overview (by Domain) > Computational Unifier. > 2. The Ψ-Field as a Negentropy Source (Information Thermodynamics): > The Ψ-Field as a Negentropy Source and the Landauer Cost of Coherence
source_formulae: P0R05956: Satisfying the Generalised Second Law (GSL): The Cost of Erasure

P0R05957: To comply with the GSL (dS<sub>Total</sub>/dt 0), this local reduction in entropy must be compensated by an increase in entropy associated with the information processing within the Psi-field itself. According to Landauer's principle, the erasure of information incurs an unavoidable thermodynamic cost.

P0R05958: The Psi-field, particularly the optimising function of the Consilium (L15/L16), performs continuous computation (HPC optimisation). This involves the erasure of information (e.g., discarding outdated models or prediction errors). The heat dissipated (Q<sub>Erasure</sub>) by the Psi-field is bounded by the amount of information erased (I<sub>Erased</sub>):

source_formulae: P0R05961: The GSL is satisfied globally when the entropy increase due to erasure balances the local negentropy injection:
